circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: The Yas Marina Circuit, a 5. 281 km track designed by Hermann Tilke on Abu Dhabi's Yas Island, has served as the venue for Formula One's season-closing Abu Dhabi Grand Prix since 2009. The circuit represents the second Middle Eastern Formula One venue to be established, following Bahrain's circuit and preceding those built in Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: The Albert Park Circuit is a street track that encircles Albert Park Lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb and holds an FIA Grade 1 classification. It serves as the venue for Formula One's Australian Grand Prix each year, alongside various supporting racing series including the Supercars Championship event.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Austria, this 4. 326 km circuit originally opened as Österreichring and staged the Austrian Grand Prix annually between 1970 and 1987. Following a reconstruction and shortening, the venue was relaunched under a new name and returned to hosting the Austrian Grand Prix from 1997 through 2003.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street course in Azerbaijan measuring 6. 003 km, ranking as the fourth-longest track in Formula One racing. The venue first hosted Formula One competition in 2016 with the European Grand Prix before becoming home to the Azerbaijan Grand Prix from 2017 onwards.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, the Bahrain International Circuit is a 5. 412 km venue that hosted the inaugural Formula One race held in the Middle East that year. Beyond Formula One, the track has accommodated various motorsport categories including drag racing, GP2 Series, and 24-hour endurance competitions, while briefly featuring Australian V8 Supercars from 2006 to 2010.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya spans 4. 657 kilometers and features a versatile design combining extended straightaways with diverse corner configurations, making it suitable for testing comprehensive racing performance. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates over 140,000 spectators across its grandstand facilities.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: Located in the Walloon region of Belgium near the town of Spa, this 7. 004 km circuit has been a Formula One fixture since 1950, when it hosted a race during the championship's inaugural season. The venue has continuously staged the Belgian Grand Prix annually since 1985, with only brief absences in 2003 and 2006.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: This 4. 361 km track on Notre Dame Island in Montreal serves as the home of Canada's Formula One Grand Prix. Beyond F1, the circuit has accommodated various international racing series including sports car championships and North American open-wheel and stock car competitions.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: The Shanghai International Circuit, located in Shanghai's Jiading District, is a motorsport venue that hosted the Chinese Grand Prix annually between 2004 and 2019, with the event returning from 2024. The track additionally began hosting the Shanghai ePrix from 2024 onwards.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Silverstone Circuit, situated in England near villages in Northamptonshire, has been the primary venue for the British Grand Prix since 1948 and notably hosted the inaugural World Championship race in 1950. After alternating with two other circuits between 1955 and 1986, the Grand Prix became a permanent fixture at Silverstone from 1987 onwards, while the track also regularly stages the motorcycle racing world championship's British round.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring, a 4. 381 km circuit located near Budapest in Hungary, hosted the inaugural Formula One Grand Prix held in the Eastern Bloc when racing commenced there in 1986. Built in just eight months starting from late 1985, it was constructed outside the city near a major highway after plans for a Monaco-style street circuit in Budapest's central park were rejected by the government.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: Located near Monza, north of Milan, this 5. 793 km Italian racing venue dates back to 1922, making it Europe's oldest purpose-built circuit on the mainland. The Italian Grand Prix has been held there continuously since 1949, except for a closure in 1980 for renovations, earning the track its "Temple of Speed" nickname through its distinctive long straights and high-velocity turns.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit is a 5. 807 km Japanese motorsport venue in Mie Prefecture operated by Honda Mobilityland, hosting up to 155,000 spectators. The track is primarily recognized as a prestigious Formula One grand prix location and also serves as a key venue for the Japanese Super Formula championship.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street course that winds through Paradise, Nevada, featuring some of the city's most iconic venues and landmarks such as the MSG Sphere, Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas. The track follows Las Vegas Boulevard, the famous strip renowned for its major hotels and casinos, creating a unique urban racing environment amid the city's most recognizable attractions.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: Located in Mexico City, the 4. 304 km Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez is named after two Mexican racing brothers who died in accidents, Ricardo in 1962 and Pedro in 1971. The circuit has hosted Formula One's Mexican Grand Prix intermittently, most recently from 2015 onwards under its current "Mexico City Grand Prix" designation, though it previously held the race on a different layout until 1992.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary circuit constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, featuring a 3. 363-mile layout with 19 corners that produces qualifying speeds averaging 139 mph. Designed by specialist track architects Apex Circuit Design, the venue was established to host the Miami Grand Prix, which debuted on the championship calendar in 2022.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco stretches 3. 337 kilometers through the streets of Monte Carlo and La Condamine, weaving around Monaco's harbor. Though officially recognized by its formal name, it is widely known as the Monte Carlo circuit due to its location within that district.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: This 4. 259-kilometre track sits in the Dutch coastal dunes near Amsterdam and hosted the Dutch Grand Prix after returning to Formula One in 2021. The venue will conclude its current F1 partnership after the 2026 season.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: The Lusail International Circuit sits near Lusail, north of Doha in Qatar, spanning 5. 419 kilometres with a 1. 068-kilometre main straight and holding an FIA Grade 1 license.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Located in São Paulo, Brazil, Interlagos is a 4. 309 km counterclockwise circuit that opened in 1940 and was renamed in 1985 to commemorate Formula 1 driver José Carlos Pace, who perished in an aviation accident eight years prior. The venue hosts prestigious international racing events and features associated karting facilities named after another renowned Brazilian racing legend.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: The Jeddah Corniche Circuit is a street track positioned along the Red Sea waterfront in Saudi Arabia, serving as the venue for the Saudi Arabian Grand Prix in Formula One since its inaugural hosting in 2021. The facility has expanded its motorsport calendar to include international electric racing events beginning in 2025.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit is a street-based racing venue situated around Marina Bay in Singapore, with its layout spanning through the Downtown Core and Kallang districts.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, this 4. 657-kilometre track features a balanced mix of lengthy straights and diverse corner types, making it a versatile test for modern racing machinery. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and can accommodate approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: Located in Austin, Texas, the Circuit of the Americas is a 3. 426-mile FIA Grade 1 facility that serves as the permanent home of the Formula One United States Grand Prix. Beyond F1, the track hosts major international racing events including MotoGP's Motorcycle Grand Prix of the Americas and the Lone Star Le Mans endurance race, alongside various other motorsport series.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon is a Thai-British racing driver who represents Thailand in Formula One and currently drives for Williams.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Spanish driver Fernando Alonso currently races for Aston Martin in Formula One, where he has claimed two World Drivers' Championships during his 23-season career with 32 Grand Prix victories. Beyond single-seaters, Alonso has achieved remarkable success in endurance racing, including two Le Mans 24 Hours victories and the 2018-19 World Endurance Championship, making him the sole driver to win both Formula One and World Endurance Championships.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Andrea Kimi Antonelli, an Italian driver, joined Mercedes for his Formula One career that began in 2025 and has already yielded five Grand Prix victories.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British motorsport competitor currently racing in Formula One with the Haas team.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Bortoleto is a Brazilian racing driver currently competing in Formula One for Audi, representing his country in the sport's premier championship.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Bottas, a Finnish driver competing for Cadillac in Formula One, has secured two runner-up finishes in the drivers' championship while driving for Mercedes and claimed ten race victories throughout his thirteen-season career.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Franco Alejandro Colapinto is an Argentine racing driver currently competing in Formula One with the Alpine team.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Australian driver Jack Doohan balances roles as a reserve driver for Haas in Formula One while actively competing in the European Le Mans Series' LMP2 category with Nielsen Racing. He has participated in seven Grand Prix events across the 2024 and 2025 seasons.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: Pierre Gasly is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Alpine. The Monaco-born competitor previously achieved victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix while driving for AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Alexandre Hadjar represents France in Formula One competition, where he drives for Red Bull Racing. The driver holds both French and Algerian nationality.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Ferrari who has secured seven world championship titles, equalling Michael Schumacher's record. He holds multiple career records including the most race victories, pole positions, and podium finishes in the sport's history.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: Nicolas Hülkenberg, a German motorsport competitor, currently drives for Audi in Formula One. Beyond circuit racing, he achieved success in endurance competition, securing victory at the prestigious 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 while piloting a Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson is a New Zealand racing driver currently competing in Formula One with Racing Bulls.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: Charles Leclerc, a Monégasque driver competing for Ferrari, achieved second place in the 2022 drivers' championship and has secured nine race victories throughout his nine seasons in the sport. The Ferrari driver has established himself as a consistent front-running competitor in Formula One.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Kevin Magnussen is a Danish driver currently competing in the FIA World Endurance Championship with WRT as a BMW factory driver, while also pursuing a part-time NASCAR career with Trackhouse Racing. His Formula One career spanned two separate periods, from 2014 to 2020 and again from 2022 to 2024.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British Formula One driver, secured the 2025 World Drivers' Championship while competing for McLaren. Over his eight-season career, he has claimed victory in 11 Grand Prix events.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon, a French racing driver, currently competes in Formula One for Haas. He previously secured a victory at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix while driving for Alpine.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Pérez is a Mexican driver currently competing in Formula One for Cadillac, bringing extensive experience from 15 seasons in the sport. He achieved his highest championship finish as runner-up in 2023 while driving for Red Bull, and has secured six Grand Prix victories throughout his career.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Oscar Piastri is an Australian Formula One driver competing for McLaren who has secured nine Grand Prix victories since joining the grid.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Australian driver Daniel Ricciardo competed in Formula One over a 14-season period spanning 2011 to 2024, earning the nickname "the Honey Badger" during his career. He secured eight Grand Prix victories throughout his time in the sport before retiring from racing.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: George Russell, a British motorsports competitor, currently races for Mercedes in Formula One, where he has secured victories in seven Grand Prix events throughout his eight-season tenure in the sport.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz has claimed four victories during his twelve-year Formula One career and currently races for Williams.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: American driver Logan Sargeant raced in Formula One during 2023 and 2024 before transitioning to endurance racing. He currently competes in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category with Proton Competition, following prior involvement in IMSA's LMP2 competition.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian Formula One driver currently racing for Aston Martin in the sport's top racing series.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Yuki Tsunoda, a Japanese driver, has held reserve driver positions with Red Bull Racing and Racing Bulls throughout his Formula One career, which spanned from 2021 to 2025.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: Max Verstappen is a Dutch racing driver competing for Red Bull Racing who has claimed four consecutive World Drivers' Championship titles between 2021 and 2024. The 12-season veteran has secured 71 Grand Prix victories throughout his Formula One career.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu is a Chinese racing driver who currently holds a reserve position with Cadillac in Formula One. He competed as a full-time driver in the championship between 2022 and 2024, making him the sole driver from China to have participated in the sport.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: Alpine Racing competes from its Enstone base as Renault's works team, operating under the Alpine brand since 2021 following a rebranding from the Renault F1 Team identity. The squad achieved its sole victory during this era at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix with Esteban Ocon, whilst currently powering its cars with Mercedes engines and competing under a French licence until Renault's Formula One exit in 2025.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin has competed in Formula One through multiple incarnations as a British constructor. The team currently operates under AMR GP Limited and races under the Aston Martin Aramco Formula One Team banner.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari, Italy's historic racing division, has participated in every Formula One World Championship season since 1950, making it both the sport's longest-standing and most accomplished team. The squad, nicknamed after its distinctive emblem, represents the pinnacle of Ferrari's motorsport heritage and remains a dominant force in competitive racing.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: An American Formula One outfit founded by NASCAR entrepreneur Gene Haas, the team commenced racing in 2016 and has operated with Ayao Komatsu as principal since early 2024, following the departure of long-serving leader Guenther Steiner.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: Founded in 1970 by Peter Sauber, the Swiss racing operation initially distinguished itself through endurance competition, securing two world championships and victory at the 1989 Le Mans 24 Hours alongside Mercedes-Benz before transitioning to Formula One in 1993. Operating as an independent constructor for three decades, the team competed under various identities through successive ownership changes until departing the grid in 2025 as motorsport's fourth-longest-running chassis manufacturer by race entries. Audi's acquisition of the organization in 2024 established the technical foundation for its own entry into the sport.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren Racing Limited operates as a British Formula One outfit headquartered at its Technology Centre in Woking, Surrey. The team functions as part of the McLaren Group, which is fully owned by Mumtalakat Holding Company.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes has competed in Formula One since 1954 as both a constructor and engine supplier, with the current team based in Brackley operating under a German licence. Since 2022, ownership has been shared equally among Mercedes-Benz Group, Ineos, and team principal Toto Wolff. The team is traditionally known as the "Silver Arrows".

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that began competing in 2024, operating as one of two Red Bull-owned squads alongside Red Bull Racing. Based in Faenza with a secondary facility in Milton Keynes, the team functions as a sister operation within the Austrian conglomerate's motorsport portfolio.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that began competing in 2024 as part of Red Bull GmbH's two-team operation alongside Red Bull Racing. Headquartered in Faenza with a secondary facility in Milton Keynes, the team functions as Red Bull's sister squad in the championship.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing operates as an Austrian-licensed Formula One team headquartered in England and part of the Red Bull GmbH conglomerate. Established in 2005 and led by Christian Horner until his 2025 departure, the team is now directed by Laurent Mekies. Red Bull GmbH also owns Racing Bulls, making Red Bull Racing one of two Formula One operations under the conglomerate's ownership.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering is a British Formula One constructor founded in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head, succeeding Williams's earlier failed racing venture. Headquartered in Grove, Oxfordshire, the team competes under the Atlassian Williams banner in 2026 and remains one of the sport's established operations.
